title: aeschynomene katangensis subsp. sublignosa
intro: Aeschynomene katangensis subsp. sublignosa, also known as the Aeschynomene katangensis sublignosa plant, is a species of plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is a medicinal plant used in traditional medicine, particularly in Central Africa, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as the promotion of wound healing and the management of respiratory problems. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant is often used in infusions or decoctions, and may also be dried and powdered for use in capsules or other medicinal products.
